topic.what: Automated cross-chain smart contract auditing framework using graph-of-thought reasoning with static analysis and LLM synergy
topic.how: Five-layer audit graph architecture that emulates expert auditor methodology by combining static data-flow extraction with constrained LLM reasoning for predicate verification and bypass analysis
topic.why_matters: Cross-chain bridges have caused $2.8B+ losses; existing tools lack semantic understanding of cross-chain interactions and struggle with multi-contract dependencies

characteristics.what_it_measures: Coverage of fine-grained audit points in benchmark
characteristics.evidence: Table III - 270 TP out of 294 annotated audit points

characteristics.what_it_measures: Detection of vulnerable projects in benchmark
characteristics.evidence: Table VI - 19 out of 20 vulnerable projects detected

characteristics.context: Harmonic mean of precision and recall

characteristics.what_itasures: Fraction of reported issues that are valid
characteristics.context: Trade-off with recall for practical deployment

characteristics.what_it_measures: LLM inference cost for full audit
characteristics.evidence: Table VII - average 62k input + 8.5k output tokens
characteristics.context: 62,208 input tokens, 8,543 output tokens per project

characteristics.what_it_measures: Time to complete full audit per project
characteristics.context: Includes static analysis layers and LLM inference

characteristics.what_itasures: Confirmed risks in production cross-chain projects
characteristics.evidence: Table VIII - 116 missing constraints, 12 bypass paths, 108+9 confirmed

novelty: Graph of Auditing Thoughts reasoning architecture
Cross-chain Token Transaction Verification Framework with formal predicates
Hybrid SA+LLM pipeline constraining LLM reasoning within structural boundaries
open_problems: Implicit semantic dependencies (e.g., implicit slippage calculations)
Limited to projects with explicit cross-chain event patterns
Single FN case in Allbridge due to implicit arithmetic patterns
Potential over-flagging of defensive programming deviations
